metadata.raw_text: {"scores": {"clarity": 5, "simplicity": 5, "brevity_economy": 4, "structure_flow": 5, "specificity_precision": 4, "humanity_voice": 5, "overall": 5}, "rationale": "Exceptionally clear and well-organized explainer. The credit card and mortgage analogies are apt and immediately grounding. Language is genuinely plain without being dumbed down. Structure flows naturally from definition to why cities issue them to why investors buy them—a logical arc. Specificity is good with concrete examples (schools, bridges, airports) and mention of tax exemption details, though it could have briefly noted the distinction between general obligation and revenue bonds for added precision. rationale: Exceptionally clear and well-organized explainer. The credit card and mortgage analogies are apt and immediately grounding. Language is genuinely plain without being dumbed down. Structure flows naturally from definition to why cities issue them to why investors buy them—a logical arc. Specificity is good with concrete examples (schools, bridges, airports) and mention of tax exemption details, though it could have briefly noted the distinction between general obligation and revenue bonds for added precision. Slight redundancy in the closing summary restating what was already clear, costing a point on brevity. Voice is warm, conversational, and reader-aware throughout without feeling forced.
